page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8369B processors running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. With a 1:1 ratio of physical cores to vCPUs and no hyperthreading, each of the 128 vCPUs maps directly to a physical core, providing highly predictable compute performance. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. To support high-throughput data transfers and network-bound operations, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps. This high network capacity helps mitigate the lack of local storage by ensuring fast access to remote volumes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7.32xlarge offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory footprint. This makes it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory allocations or local NVMe drives. The dedicated core allocation ensures that performance remains consistent without resource contention from other tenants.

The hardware profile is optimized for compute-intensive workloads. Suitable use cases include high-performance computing, batch processing, scientific modeling, large-scale web serving, and distributed analytics where raw CPU power and high network throughput are prioritized over local storage speed or massive memory-to-CPU ratios.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8369B processors with an x86_64 architecture, it provides a 1:1 ratio of physical cores to vCPUs for predictable, non-shared performance. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps. This hardware configuration offers high compute density and qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks, minimizing memory overhead. It is designed for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, large-scale web serving, and distributed analytics.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high-speed network connectivity for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
